name: ADHD phenotype algorithm
description: This test case evaluates the ability to identify ADHD cases using a complex phenotype algorithm that requires: 1) patients aged 4+ years, 2) presence of ADHD/hyperkinetic disorder diagnostic codes from clinical encounters, and 3) prescription history of ADHD medications. This represents the primary case definition path (1a+1b+1c) from the PheKB ADHD phenotype algorithm, testing multi-resource queries combining patient demographics, diagnostic history, and medication prescriptions.
prompt: Find all patients who are at least 4 years old with a documented diagnosis of ADHD or hyperkinetic disorder and have been prescribed ADHD medications.

required_codes.display: Attention deficit disorder without mention of hyperactivity
Attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder, predominantly inattentive type
Attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder, predominantly hyperactive type
